description_long: This is a streamlined, single-engine airplane with a smooth, rounded fuselage and a classic mid-20th-century design. The wings are mid-mounted and straight, extending symmetrically from the body with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The tail section features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a subtle canopy outline. The landing gear is visible and consists of two main wheels and a smaller tail wheel, indicating a taildragger configuration. The surface is uniformly colored and appears metallic, with minimal surface detailing or markings.
description: A streamlined, single-engine airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and a front-enclosed cockpit. The surface is smooth and metallic, with visible taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, mid-mounted wings, and the single vertical stabilizer at the tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded nose, symmetrical wing extension, and the cockpit canopy at the front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the taildragger landing gear and tail section.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the horizontal stabilizers, single vertical stabilizer, and the full wingspan.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a single tail wheel, fixed gear
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a streamlined canopy
